pattern: Because popcount(a OR b) + popcount(a AND b) equals popcount(a) + popcount(b), the condition depends only on each distinct value's bit count, reducing the pair count to a sorted-popcount two-pointer tally.
hints: The set-bit sum of OR and AND of two numbers equals the sum of their individual set-bit counts.
So only distinct values matter, and each is represented solely by its popcount; you need pairs of distinct values whose popcounts sum to at least k.
Deduplicate, compute each value's popcount, sort those counts, then for each count two-pointer or binary-search how many others reach k.

edge_inputs.description: small example with several excellent pairs
k too high for any pair
single distinct value pairs with itself
k equals 1 so every ordered distinct pair counts
duplicates collapse to distinct values before counting
value with itself just reaches k

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**9) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 60)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: deduplication and popcount distribution drive the count
